title: The Ormulum
summary: The repository artifact is a local copy/version of University of Michigan CME item AFW5744.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding linked to 1,104 page images of the two-volume 1878 Clarendon Press edition. Robert Holt's 1878 edition revised Robert Meadows White's 1852 edition and newly collated its text against the principal and autograph witness, Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Junius 1. The complete manuscript is publicly digitized by the Bodleian. Jan van Vliet's partial seventeenth-century transcript in Lambeth Palace Library MS 783 is not an independent medieval witness and was not a source for White/Holt; it is recorded because it preserves passages copied before leaves disappeared and is used selectively in the 2023 Johannesson–Cooper comparison edition.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for afw5744
entities.description: The checked-in XML source used by this repository, preserving U-M's bibliographic header, uncorrected OCR declaration, title pages, page-break references, and selected textual content from the 1878 edition.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AFW5744.0001.001
entities.description: A U-M digital text published in 2006, produced by uncorrected OCR and encoded at TEI in Libraries Level 1. It transcribes the title pages, errata, dedication, 242-item text list, verse preface, and surviving Middle English body while marking most modern front matter and the notes/glossary as omitted; 1,104 digital page images are linked by PB references.
notes: The item-level XML says explicitly that the OCR was not corrected and the content was not edited. This takes precedence over the collection-level description of the 2000 batch as generally re-keyed and proofread.

entities.label: University of Michigan 600 dpi scan of the 1878 White/Holt edition
entities.description: A 1,104-image scan of both volumes, made in 2006 and distributed through U-M page views and an Internet Archive item. Internet Archive supplies PDF, DjVu, OCR, and source-derivative downloads.
entities.reproduction_format: 1,104 600 dpi page images; Internet Archive Text PDF and DjVu derivatives

entities.label: White–Holt 1878 edition of The Ormulum
alternate_labels: The Ormulum, with the Notes and Glossary of Dr. R. M. White
entities.description: Robert Holt's revised two-volume edition. It retains White's text, notes, glossary, and long preface while correcting the text by fresh collation with the Bodleian manuscript and revising or extending the notes and glossary.

bibliographic.citation: The Ormulum, with the Notes and Glossary of Dr. R. M. White, edited by Robert Holt, 2 vols. (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1878).
bibliographic.title: The Ormulum, with the Notes and Glossary of Dr. R. M. White

notes: Dekker identifies specific places where van Vliet's seventeenth-century alterations in Junius 1 entered White's and Holt's printed text. That indirect transmission through the altered principal witness is not evidence that either editor consulted Lambeth Palace Library MS 783.

entities.label: Robert Meadows White's 1852 edition of The Ormulum
alternate_labels: The Ormulum, Now First Edited from the Original Manuscript in the Bodleian
entities.description: The first complete scholarly edition, prepared by Robert Meadows White from the original Bodleian manuscript with notes and a glossary; it became the basis of Holt's 1878 revision.

bibliographic.citation: The Ormulum, Now First Edited from the Original Manuscript in the Bodleian, with Notes and a Glossary by Robert Meadows White, 2 vols. (Oxford: University Press, 1852).
bibliographic.title: The Ormulum

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Junius 1
entities.description: The principal and autograph medieval witness. It preserves surviving portions of the intended first volume, including biblical narratives and homilies, with extensive insertions, deletions, and revisions in Orm's hand. A second hand added Latin pericopes and a few English additions. The manuscript is unfinished, extremely lacunose, and lacks a substantial ending.

physical_description.extent: [i] + ii + 117 + [i] leaves; manuscript proper fols. 3–118 and [119]
physical_description.format: Generally two columns, usually 50–70 lines per page
notes: Bound in modern pulp boards; the binding probably postdates significant losses suffered after Junius numbered the columns.
This is the only known medieval manuscript of the Ormulum and the upstream witness for White and Holt.

Jan van Vliet later annotated and altered this physical manuscript. Those interventions must be distinguished from Orm's autograph text and from van Vliet's separate notebook now cataloged as Lambeth MS 783.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of MS Junius 1
entities.description: The complete Digital Bodleian object for MS Junius 1, represented by a 263-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 manifest running from the upper cover through the tail.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with 263 canvases and IIIF Image API services
notes: Use this object for the complete facsimile. Digital Bodleian object 8d37b5d4-27ec-4da0-8a9e-dc3cba62c212 has only two older 35mm images.

entities.label: Bodleian catalog description of MS Junius 1
entities.description: The holding institution's Western Medieval Manuscripts catalog record, also embedded in the complete Digital Bodleian manifest metadata.

entities.label: London, Lambeth Palace Library, MS 783 (van Vliet Ormulum excerpts)
entities.description: An early-modern miscellany containing 49 leaves of Ormulum excerpts and glossaries copied by Jan van Vliet, principally on ff. 51r–88r. The six relevant sections include miscellaneous excerpts, the Dedication, the verse preface, passages from the opening homily, and material from columns now lost. It is a partial seventeenth-century transcript of Junius 1, not an independent medieval witness.

holding.notes: The current public catalog search did not expose a stable item-specific record during this review; use the exact shelfmark in an enquiry.

notes: No relation from the 1852 or 1878 edition to this entity is asserted. Although selected van Vliet alterations made directly in Junius 1 entered White/Holt, the separate Lambeth transcript was brought to modern editorial attention only in the 1930s.
The 2023 edition uses only the Lambeth transcript's evidence for known missing portions and related readings; the principal surviving text remains the autograph Junius 1.

entities.label: Johannesson–Cooper 2023 edition of Ormulum
alternate_labels: Ormulum, edited from Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Junius 1 and London, Lambeth Palace Library, MS 783
entities.description: A new two-volume scholarly edition based on a fresh transcription of Junius 1 and selective recovery of all known missing portions preserved by van Vliet in Lambeth MS 783. It is a related comparison source, not upstream of the repository artifact.

bibliographic.citation: Ormulum, edited from Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Junius 1 and London, Lambeth Palace Library, MS 783, by Nils-Lennart Johannesson and Andrew Cooper, 2 vols., EETS Original Series 360–361 (Oxford: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society, 2023).

bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 360–361
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

agents.name: Orm
notes: Medieval author and principal scribe of the autograph witness.

agents.name: Jan van Vliet
notes: Owner and early reader of Junius 1, copyist of the Ormulum excerpts in Lambeth MS 783, and annotator who also altered the principal manuscript.

access.access_method: Read the checked-out CME submodule path.
Read and search the structured text online; use the item contents and page links to navigate the encoded portions.
access.format: HTML-rendered OCR/TEI text with linked page images

access.access_method: Read online or download the complete combined Text PDF, DjVu, OCR text, or derivative files from Internet Archive.
access.format: Text PDF, page images, DjVu, OCR, and metadata

access.access_method: Read or download the two public scans from Internet Archive.

access.access_method: Browse all 263 canvases in Digital Bodleian or open the IIIF manifest in a compatible viewer.
notes: Free access does not authorize commercial reuse; the item attribution specifies CC BY-NC 4.0 and the Digital Bodleian terms require separate permission for commercial use.

access.access_method: Register as a reader, book a reading-room place, and order shelfmark MS 783 at least two working days in advance; confirm availability with the Library.
Use the Library enquiry route to request images of MS 783, specifying the Ormulum material on ff. 51r–88r; ask for a quote and written reuse terms. Publication permission uses a separate signed form.
access.format: Digital images on request, subject to availability and terms

access.access_method: Order the two-volume set through Oxford University Press or locate it by ISBN 9780192890436; EETS lists it as in print/print-on-demand.
notes: The 2026 EETS list gives £130 / US$170. Prices and availability can change; verify before ordering.

rights.component: The checked-in U-M-derived OCR/TEI XML and its transcription of the 1878 edition
rights.basis: U-M currently marks AFW5744.0001.001 public domain and applies No Copyright–United States; the source edition was published in 1878. The local artifact reproduces that U-M encoding.
notes: This concerns this source artifact, not repository-authored software, metadata, typography, generated editions, Bodleian manuscript images, Lambeth-supplied images, or the 2023 scholarly edition.
No Copyright–United States is a jurisdiction-specific provider statement, not a worldwide license.

rights.component: The AFW5744.0001.001 materials presented by U-M, including the public digital text
rights.basis: The exact U-M item page says the materials are in the public domain and applies the DPLA No Copyright–United States statement.
notes: The embedded 2006 XML availability boilerplate says copyright may apply where applicable; the current item-specific public-domain statement is recorded separately rather than silently deleting that historical notice.

rights.component: The U-M page images and Internet Archive derivatives of the 1878 edition
rights.basis: U-M's exact item statement marks the materials public domain and the scanned source was published in 1878; no contrary item-specific rights restriction was found in Internet Archive metadata.

rights.component: The 1878 edition's text, notes, glossary, and other published editorial matter
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1878, and U-M expressly marks its digitized item public domain in the United States.

rights.component: The 1852 edition's text, notes, glossary, and other published editorial matter
rights.basis: The edition was first published in 1852, centuries before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: The medieval textual content embodied in the autograph manuscript
rights.basis: The late-twelfth-century work and manuscript are centuries beyond any U.S. copyright term.
notes: This does not apply to modern Bodleian photography, catalog prose, interface assets, or provider terms.

rights.component: The complete Digital Bodleian photographic image set
rights.jurisdiction: Provider license and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The item attribution states Photo © Bodleian Libraries, University of Oxford and applies CC BY-NC 4.0. Digital Bodleian's terms permit non-commercial reuse with attribution and require permission and possible fees for commercial use.
notes: Linking to the viewer or IIIF manifest is distinct from redistributing the image files.
A repository tied to commercial print-on-demand activity should not redistribute these images or use them in commercial products without written permission from imaging@bodleian.ox.ac.uk.

rights.component: Any manuscript images supplied by Lambeth Palace Library in response to a reproduction order
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The Library's permission form says it does not own copyright in all holdings, limits permission to the stated use, may charge reproduction fees, requires acknowledgement, and treats additional formats or revised editions as separately licensed.
notes: Do not add commissioned images to git or a print-on-demand workflow until item-specific written terms expressly authorize that use.
The seventeenth-century transcript's age does not by itself determine rights in newly supplied photography or contractual conditions.

rights.component: The 2023 edition's transcription, reconstruction, editorial apparatus, glossary, typography, introduction, and other modern contributions
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom, United States, and applicable publisher terms
rights.basis: This is a recently published scholarly edition offered for sale by EETS/OUP; no open license or public-domain dedication for its editorial content was found.
notes: The edition is valuable for comparison, but purchasing or consulting it does not authorize republication of its protected editorial content.

editorial_practices.description: Holt says that he carefully corrected White's text by renewed collation with the original manuscript in the Bodleian Library.
editorial_practices.scope: The Middle English text throughout the 1878 edition
notes: Not every change is marked: Holt says that corrections and additions were too numerous to identify consistently without disfiguring the book.

editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR of selected textual matter with page-break references across all 1,104 images
editorial_practices.description: U-M encodes the title pages, errata, dedication, planned-text list, verse preface, and surviving medieval text, but marks most of the long modern preface and the notes/glossary as omitted front or back matter.
notes: The XML declaration says no OCR corrections or content editing were performed.

evidence.locator: HEADER, TITLEPAGE, TEXT, and PB sequence
evidence.summary: The local XML identifies AFW5744.0001.001; records U-M, 2006, 1,104 600 dpi page images, and the 1878 White/Holt source; declares uncorrected OCR and TEI in Libraries Level 1; links the page images; and contains selected front matter and the surviving medieval text.

evidence.citation: Structural and element inspection of the checked-in afw5744 XML, performed 2026-07-11.
evidence.summary: Inspection found 1,104 PB, 8 DIV1, 264 DIV2, 159 LG, and 20,531 L elements. The encoded medieval content comprises a 342-line dedication, 242-item Latin plan, 106-line preface, and 20,083-line body divided into 22 XML items, ending fragmentarily after John XXXII.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, The Ormulum, AFW5744.0001.001.
evidence.locator: Item information, rights, pages, and contents
evidence.summary: The exact item page identifies the White/Holt 1878 Clarendon Press edition, exposes the OCR text and pages, and marks the materials public domain under No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, About the Middle English Compendium.
evidence.locator: Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse; Growth of the Corpus; Copyright Policies
evidence.summary: The collection history says Ormulum belonged to the expanded group of texts and describes the corpus generally as re-keyed and structurally marked up from public-domain editions. This collection-level account conflicts in method and date detail with the item-specific 2006 uncorrected-OCR declaration.

evidence.citation: Robert Holt, ed., The Ormulum, with the Notes and Glossary of Dr. R. M. White, 2 vols. (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1878), complete U-M scan.
evidence.locator: Title pages and Preface to the New Edition, vol. 1, pp. v–vi
evidence.summary: The title pages establish Holt as editor of a work carrying White's notes and glossary. Holt's preface says White first edited from the original Bodleian manuscript, and that Holt corrected the text by renewed manuscript collation while revising and enlarging the notes and glossary.

evidence.citation: Robert Meadows White, ed., The Ormulum, Now First Edited from the Original Manuscript in the Bodleian, 2 vols. (Oxford: University Press, 1852), Internet Archive scans.
evidence.locator: Complete volumes 1 and 2, including title pages
evidence.summary: The scans confirm White's two-volume 1852 edition and its stated derivation from the original Bodleian manuscript; Holt's 1878 preface identifies this as the former edition he revised.

The metadata identifies U-M as contributor and sponsor, 1878 as source date, 2006 as scan date, 600 ppi, 1,104 images, ABBYY FineReader 8 OCR, and downloadable PDF, DjVu, OCR, and metadata derivatives.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS Junius 1, complete Digital Bodleian object and IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.summary: The official metadata identifies The Orrmulum, Orm as author and scribe, Middle English and Latin, date 1170–1185, autograph status, extreme lacunae, physical extent and layout, proposed Bourne provenance, and the full sequence of manuscript images.
notes: The complete object has 263 canvases. A different Digital Bodleian object, 8d37b5d4-27ec-4da0-8a9e-dc3cba62c212, has only two 35mm images and must not be linked as the complete facsimile.

evidence.citation: University of Oxford Faculty of English, The Orrmulum: Bodleian MS Junius 1.
evidence.summary: Oxford confirms that the sole manuscript is completely digitized and describes the work as a vast unfinished Gospel translation written by the Augustinian canon Orrm in the 1160s and 1170s to help priests preach in English.

evidence.locator: Permitted use; prohibited use; commercial licensing
evidence.summary: The terms explain that most images use CC BY-NC 4.0 with attribution, allow non-commercial academic and research reuse within stated limits, prohibit unlicensed commercial uses, and direct high-resolution or commercial licensing enquiries to imaging@bodleian.ox.ac.uk.

evidence.citation: Middle English Compendium Bibliography, Ormulum, BIB2677.
evidence.locator: Manuscript, edition, and MED stencil records
evidence.summary: The record distinguishes Junius 1, Holt 1878, Johannesson–Cooper 2023, and van Vliet's partial transcript in Lambeth 783; dates the work c.1175; and warns that the 2023 edition partly normalizes Vliet-derived text to Orm's usage without comment.

evidence.citation: Stockholm University Department of English, The Ormulum, 20 November 2023.
evidence.summary: The Ormulum Project describes the work, its East Midlands language, Orm's autograph Junius 1, the fresh transcription, the selective recovery of all known missing portions from van Vliet's Lambeth copy, and publication through EETS/OUP.

evidence.citation: Early English Text Society, Publications and List of All Publications 2026, OS 360–361.
evidence.locator: Recent Publications and entry for OS 360–361
evidence.summary: EETS identifies Johannesson and Cooper's Ormulum as Original Series 360–361, published 2023 for 2022, ISBN 9780192890436, and lists the set at £130 / US$170 in its 2026 price list; the publications page directs non-members to OUP for orders.

evidence.citation: Kees Dekker, The Ormulum in the Seventeenth Century: The Manuscript and Its Early Readers, Neophilologus 102 (2018): 257–277.
evidence.locator: Manuscript; Lambeth 783 sections; spelling and emendations
evidence.summary: Dekker reconstructs the provenance and losses of Junius 1; identifies 49 leaves of van Vliet Ormulum excerpts and glossaries in Lambeth 783; maps six transcript sections across ff. 51r–88r; and documents specific van Vliet alterations in Junius 1 that later appeared in White/Holt.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library, Using the Library.
evidence.locator: Registering and booking, enquiries, opening hours, and contact
evidence.summary: The Library explains reader registration, advance booking and ordering, provides an online enquiry route, and lists archives@churchofengland.org and +44 20 7898 1400.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Reprographic Services, Application for Permission to Reproduce Images.
evidence.summary: The form allows a user to request images and permission, but makes authorization use-specific, potentially fee-bearing, non-transferable, and conditional on attribution; it says additional formats or revised editions need separate permission.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.locator: Published works and public-domain term chart
evidence.summary: The Copyright Office term chart supports the U.S. public-domain status in 2026 of editions first published in 1852 and 1878.

review_notes: The exact current complete manuscript object is Digital Bodleian 90a06f70-880a-4b5b-bd30-798710afff11 with 263 canvases. The superficially similar 8d37b5d4-27ec-4da0-8a9e-dc3cba62c212 record contains only two 35mm images.

Lambeth MS 783 is deliberately modeled as a partial seventeenth-century derivative transcript of Junius 1. No upstream relation from White 1852, Holt 1878, the U-M encoding, or the repository artifact to Lambeth 783 is asserted.

Some van Vliet readings nevertheless entered White/Holt indirectly because van Vliet altered the physical Junius 1 manuscript before White and Holt transcribed and collated it. That limited contamination is recorded in notes and evidence rather than turning Lambeth 783 into an editorial source.

The item-specific 2006 XML production statement says uncorrected OCR; the generic U-M collection narrative says the broader batch was re-keyed and proofread. The item-specific evidence governs this record while the discrepancy remains open.

open_questions.question: What exact U-M release, download date, and transformation history produced the pinned local afw5744 XML blob?
next_steps: Compare the local file byte-for-byte with any current U-M bulk XML download and older archived releases.
Inspect CME repository history and surviving U-M production documentation for an ingestion date or transformation log.

open_questions.question: How should the item-level 2006 uncorrected-OCR statement be reconciled with U-M's collection-level account of Ormulum as part of a generally re-keyed and proofread corpus expansion?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections whether AFW5744 was a later replacement or OCR exception within the earlier corpus batch.
Preserve both dated descriptions and do not infer correction or accuracy from the generic collection narrative.

open_questions.question: Is any complete or partial public digital facsimile of Lambeth Palace Library MS 783 available, and what item-specific reuse terms would apply to requested images?
next_steps: Send the Library an enquiry citing MS 783 and ff. 51r–88r, asking whether existing digital images can be supplied.
Request a quote, resolution and file-format details, and written permission terms for repository and print-on-demand contexts before ordering.
Record any supplied facsimile as a new entity with checksums and item-specific terms; do not commit the images unless redistribution is explicitly authorized.
notes: No public facsimile was found through the current Lambeth catalog, image search, or institutional pages checked on the review date; this is negative search evidence, not proof that no internal digitization exists.
